label: Test-time compute scaling

node.label: Test-time compute scaling
node.definition: Test-time compute scaling spends additional inference effort on sampling, search, verification, revision, tools, or longer deliberation instead of changing model weights.
node.why_it_matters: It creates a second scaling axis in which difficult instances receive more computation than easy ones.
limitations: Extra tokens do not guarantee better reasoning, latency and cost rise quickly, and stopping rules can be poorly calibrated.

dependencies.rationale: Test-time compute scaling explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Search, sampling & decoding procedures.
Test-time compute scaling explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Self-consistency & best-of-N inference.
Test-time compute scaling explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops.
In this atlas graph, Test-time compute scaling has a `searches_with` dependency on Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives with `common` strength.

sentence_units.text: Test-time compute scaling spends additional inference effort on sampling, search, verification, revision, tools, or longer deliberation instead of changing model weights.
It creates a second scaling axis in which difficult instances receive more computation than easy ones.
Extra tokens do not guarantee better reasoning, latency and cost rise quickly, and stopping rules can be poorly calibrated.
